# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18

# Set a non-root user
RUN useradd -ms /bin/bash appuser

# Install system utilities and dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    curl \
    git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files and install dependencies
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install --production

# Copy application files
COPY . .

# Change ownership of the app files to the non-root user
RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app

# Switch to the non-root user
USER appuser

# Expose the application port
EXPOSE 3000

# Start the application
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
